exercises.title: HUNTERS ABOARD
exercises.prompt: Join **crew** to **ships** on crew.ship_id = ships.id. Return each **hunter's name** and their **ship's name** (two columns), keeping only rows where the crew member's **role** is **'hunter'**. Sort by crew **name** (A->Z).
exercises.setup: CREATE TABLE ships (id INTEGER, name TEXT);INSERT INTO ships VALUES (1,'Bebop'),(2,'Swordfish');CREATE TABLE crew (name TEXT, ship_id INTEGER, role TEXT);INSERT INTO crew VALUES ('Spike',1,'hunter'),('Jet',1,'captain'),('Faye',1,'hunter'),('Ed',1,'hacker');
exercises.starter: SELECT crew.name, ships.name FROM crew JOIN ships ON crew.ship_id = ships.id ORDER BY crew.name;
exercises.solution: SELECT crew.name, ships.name FROM crew JOIN ships ON crew.ship_id = ships.id WHERE crew.role = 'hunter' ORDER BY crew.name;
exercises.hint: Add WHERE crew.role = 'hunter' to the JOIN so only the bounty hunters show.
exercises.lore: Two trigger fingers, one deck. The hunters log their berth.

exercises.title: HEADCOUNT BY HULL
exercises.brief: How many hands each ship carries.
exercises.prompt: Join **crew** to **ships** on crew.ship_id = ships.id, then return each **ship's name** and the **number of crew** aboard it, one row **per ship that has crew**. Sort by ship **name** (A->Z).
exercises.setup: CREATE TABLE ships (id INTEGER, name TEXT);INSERT INTO ships VALUES (1,'Bebop'),(2,'Swordfish');CREATE TABLE crew (name TEXT, ship_id INTEGER, role TEXT);INSERT INTO crew VALUES ('Spike',1,'hunter'),('Jet',1,'captain'),('Faye',1,'hunter'),('Ed',1,'hacker');
exercises.starter: SELECT ships.name FROM crew JOIN ships ON crew.ship_id = ships.id ORDER BY ships.name;
exercises.solution: SELECT ships.name, COUNT(*) FROM crew JOIN ships ON crew.ship_id = ships.id GROUP BY ships.name ORDER BY ships.name;
exercises.hint: After the JOIN, GROUP BY ships.name so COUNT(*) gives one tally per ship.
exercises.lore: The manifest tallies itself, hull by hull.

exercises.title: BOARD THE SWORDFISH
exercises.brief: Sign a pilot onto the empty ship.
exercises.prompt:
The **Swordfish** (ship id **2**) flies with no crew. INSERT a new row into **crew**: name **Vicious**, ship_id **2**, role **pilot**. Add nobody else.

The verifier then runs: SELECT name, role FROM crew WHERE ship_id = 2.
exercises.setup: CREATE TABLE ships (id INTEGER, name TEXT);INSERT INTO ships VALUES (1,'Bebop'),(2,'Swordfish');CREATE TABLE crew (name TEXT, ship_id INTEGER, role TEXT);INSERT INTO crew VALUES ('Spike',1,'hunter'),('Jet',1,'captain'),('Faye',1,'hunter'),('Ed',1,'hacker');
exercises.check: SELECT name, role FROM crew WHERE ship_id = 2;
exercises.starter: -- INSERT the new pilot onto ship 2 here
exercises.solution: INSERT INTO crew (name, ship_id, role) VALUES ('Vicious', 2, 'pilot');
exercises.hint: INSERT INTO crew (name, ship_id, role) VALUES ('Vicious', 2, 'pilot');
exercises.lore: The lone fighter finally takes a hand at the stick.

exercises.title: EMPTY HULLS
exercises.brief: Every ship and its crew tally, even the empty ones.
exercises.prompt: Use a **LEFT JOIN** from **ships** to **crew** on ships.id = crew.ship_id so that **every ship appears** — including ones with no crew. Return each **ship's name** and the **number of crew** aboard (use COUNT on a crew column so empty ships show **0**), one row per ship. Sort by ship **name** (A->Z).
exercises.setup: CREATE TABLE ships (id INTEGER, name TEXT);INSERT INTO ships VALUES (1,'Bebop'),(2,'Swordfish');CREATE TABLE crew (name TEXT, ship_id INTEGER, role TEXT);INSERT INTO crew VALUES ('Spike',1,'hunter'),('Jet',1,'captain'),('Faye',1,'hunter'),('Ed',1,'hacker');
exercises.starter: SELECT ships.name, COUNT(*) FROM ships JOIN crew ON ships.id = crew.ship_id GROUP BY ships.name ORDER BY ships.name;
exercises.solution: SELECT ships.name, COUNT(crew.name) FROM ships LEFT JOIN crew ON ships.id = crew.ship_id GROUP BY ships.name ORDER BY ships.name;
exercises.hint: LEFT JOIN keeps empty ships; COUNT(crew.name) counts 0 for them, while COUNT(*) would wrongly count 1.
exercises.lore: Even the ghost hull reports in: zero souls aboard.

exercises.title: FLEET TRANSFER
exercises.brief: Move every Bebop hand to the Swordfish by name.
exercises.prompt:
Command reassigns the whole Bebop crew. UPDATE **crew**: set **ship_id** to the id of the ship named **'Swordfish'** for every crew member currently assigned to the ship named **'Bebop'**. Do not hard-code the numbers 1 or 2 — look both ids up from the **ships** table with subqueries.

The verifier then runs: SELECT crew.name, ships.name FROM crew JOIN ships ON crew.ship_id = ships.id ORDER BY crew.name.
exercises.setup: CREATE TABLE ships (id INTEGER, name TEXT);INSERT INTO ships VALUES (1,'Bebop'),(2,'Swordfish');CREATE TABLE crew (name TEXT, ship_id INTEGER, role TEXT);INSERT INTO crew VALUES ('Spike',1,'hunter'),('Jet',1,'captain'),('Faye',1,'hunter'),('Ed',1,'hacker');
exercises.check: SELECT crew.name, ships.name FROM crew JOIN ships ON crew.ship_id = ships.id ORDER BY crew.name;
exercises.starter: UPDATE crew SET ship_id = (SELECT id FROM ships WHERE name = 'Swordfish') WHERE name = 'Spike';
exercises.solution: UPDATE crew SET ship_id = (SELECT id FROM ships WHERE name = 'Swordfish') WHERE ship_id = (SELECT id FROM ships WHERE name = 'Bebop');
exercises.hint: SET ship_id = (SELECT id FROM ships WHERE name = 'Swordfish') WHERE ship_id = (SELECT id FROM ships WHERE name = 'Bebop').
exercises.lore: Papers stamped: the whole deck wakes up on a different ship.
